reasoning: The statistical yfinance model predicts a 0.47 probability of the price being above the 2026-04-12 threshold of 31.70, dropping to 0.40 when seasonality is accounted for. The short-term trend is slightly negative (3 negative days out of the last 4 leading up to the cutoff date). Recent earnings news, however, showed strong financial performance (beating EPS expectations and guiding $1.11B in free cash flow), implying stable long-term fundamentals. Because short-term weekly stock moves retain much of the random walk properties, I anchor the 1-week forecast near the statistical estimate at 0.43. As the forecast horizon increases to months and years, my uncertainty correctly dominates, and I bring the probabilities smoothly closer to 0.50. The median performance over multi-year horizons for single stocks generally flattens out around a coin toss for strictly price appreciation, ignoring dividends (which is perfectly in-line with the explicit instruction to have distant horizons closer to 0.5).
